prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "死虫式" (dead bug) exercise, three-quarter side view, body lying on the back, viewed from the right side at slight angle from above. Person posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a dead bug exercise. Lying SUPINE on the floor, lower back pressed flat to the ground. The body shows DIAGONAL OPPOSITE limb action: ONE side extends, the OTHER stays tucked. Specifically: the RIGHT ARM is reaching back overhead almost touching the floor behind the head (arm fully extended). The LEFT LEG is fully extended straight out forward, parallel to the floor, just a few centimeters above the ground (toes pointing forward). At the same time the LEFT ARM points straight up toward the ceiling (vertical arm), and the RIGHT LEG is held with knee tucked toward the chest (right thigh vertical/perpendicular to the floor with shin parallel to the floor like a tabletop). Show a CLEAR VISUAL DIAGONAL between the extended-right-arm-back-overhead and the extended-left-leg-forward; meanwhile the tucked left arm points up and the tucked right knee points up. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: deep core (transverse abdominis and rectus abdominis) for anti-extension. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the bent leg and shoulder stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹横肌" pointing to the transverse abdominis (deep core); "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "髂腰肌 / 肩稳定肌" pointing to the hip flexors and shoulder stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
